tag: Duplicate subscription charge and refund escalation
summary: The customer reported being charged twice for their subscription. The agent verified the customer's identity and confirmed the duplicate charge, stating that a refund is warranted. However, the agent noted that the billing tool has been unreliable since a recent outage and could not confirm immediate refund processing. The agent opened a priority ticket (case 55231), escalated the issue to billing, and promised a personal follow-up the next day to confirm resolution.
recommended_next_steps: Agent to follow up with customer on 2026-07-02 to confirm refund status.
Monitor billing tool stability and ensure refund is processed.

decisions.decision: A refund is warranted for the duplicate charge.
decisions.evidence: 5: [00:24] Agent: Thank you, that verifies you. I do see two charges on the 3rd. That's a duplicate on our end, so a refund is warranted.

action_items.task: Open a priority ticket (case 55231) and escalate to billing.
action_items.evidence: 9: [00:46] Agent: I hear you. I don't have authority to expedite the refund myself, so I'm opening a priority ticket — case 55231 — and escalating to billing. I'll personally follow up with you tomorrow to confirm it went through.

action_items.task: Personally follow up with the customer to confirm refund processed.
action_items.evidence: 9: [00:46] Agent: I hear you. I don't have authority to expedite the refund myself, so I'm opening a priority ticket — case 55231 — and escalating to billing. I'll personally follow up with you tomorrow to confirm it went through.

blockers.blocker: Billing tool instability since outage prevents confirmation of refund processing.
blockers.evidence: 7: [00:34] Agent: Normally three to five business days. I'll be honest though — our billing tool's been flaky since yesterday's outage, so I can't fully confirm it processed.

compliance_observations.note: Agent requested and received customer email and billing zip to verify identity before discussing account details and processing a refund. This is appropriate verification for the action.
